title: 顺水推舟
summary:
During an interrogation, Francis Claude denies bribing Major Potter to falsely identify the deceased man as Robert Andrich (also known as André Hercule). While she admits to wanting a share of Gordon's money through unconventional means, she denies fabricating evidence to strip Rosaline Claude of her status. Poirot informs Francis that Major Potter committed suicide that afternoon, explaining that Potter chose death rather than facing the consequences of his false testimony and the financial hardship caused by his lies.

The following morning, Poirot meets with Inspector Spence and reveals that the man posing as Enoch Yates is actually Charles Trenton, a known swindler who uses high-status appearances to facilitate theft. They discuss the dismissal of the case against David Hunter, noting that a witness, Jimmy Pierce, saw a woman in a tweed coat and an orange scarf near a phone box on the night of the crime. Poirot suggests the woman's appearance—including heavy makeup and slacks—might be a disguise used by a local resident, such as Lynn Marchmont or Madame Jeremy, to avoid recognition.

Poirot concludes that the case is fundamentally flawed because the deceased's character does not match that of André Hercule; the man in Stoke was a blackmailer, whereas André was an eccentric, chivalrous man. Poirot also expresses concern regarding the behavior of David Hunter and Rosaline Claude, noting that Rosaline appears to be afraid not just for her property, but for her life.
